name: Sandbox CLI Connector
description: Execute CLI commands securely in a sandboxed environment with nsjail isolation

properties.label: Security Profile
properties.description: Security profile that defines available tools and permissions
properties.tooltip:
Each profile is a pre-configured security policy:

- DEFAULT: Data processing only (jq, yq, grep, sed, awk). No network access. Safest option.
- DEVELOPMENT: Adds curl, git, kubectl, helm with read-only restrictions. Network enabled.
- CLOUD-OPS: Full cloud CLI access (AWS, GCP, Azure). Requires credentials. Use with caution.
- INFRA-AUTOMATION: Terraform for IaC. Plan/validate only by default.
- MONITORING: Read-only kubectl and curl for observability.

choices.name: Default - Data Processing (jq, yq, grep, sed, awk)
Development - API & Kubernetes (curl, git, kubectl, helm)
Cloud Operations - Cloud CLIs (aws, gcloud, az)

properties.label: Execution Mode
properties.description: How do you want to execute code?
properties.tooltip:
Choose how to provide the code to execute:

- COMMAND: Provide CLI arguments (e.g., jq '.key'). Best for simple one-liners and CLI tools.
- SCRIPT: Provide multi-line Python script content. Best for complex logic with multiple statements, loops, functions, etc.

properties.label: Tool
properties.description: CLI tool to execute
properties.tooltip:
Data processing tools (no network access):

- jq: JSON processor. Query/transform JSON data.
- yq: YAML processor. Same syntax as jq for YAML.
- grep: Pattern matching in text.
- sed: Stream editor for text transformations.
- awk: Text processing.
- safe-python3: Secured Python interpreter (blocked: os, subprocess, socket, etc.)

properties.label: Tool
properties.description: CLI tool to execute
properties.tooltip:
Development tools (network enabled, some restrictions):

- curl: HTTP client. Full HTTP methods.
- http: HTTPie - user-friendly HTTP client.
- jq/yq: JSON/YAML processing.
- git: Read-only operations (status, log, diff, show, branch).
- kubectl: Read-only K8s (get, describe, logs). No apply/delete.
- helm: Read-only Helm (list, status, show). No install/upgrade.
- gh: GitHub CLI for issues, PRs, repos.
- safe-python3: Secured Python interpreter.

properties.label: Tool
properties.description: CLI tool to execute
properties.tooltip:
Cloud provider CLIs (REQUIRES credentials in environment):

- aws: AWS CLI. Needs AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.
- gcloud: GCP CLI. Needs GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS.
- az: Azure CLI. Needs AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID.
- kubectl: Kubernetes. Needs KUBECONFIG.
- curl/jq: For API calls and JSON processing.

WARNING: These tools can modify cloud resources. Use with caution.

properties.label: Tool
properties.description: CLI tool to execute
properties.tooltip:
Infrastructure as Code tools:

- terraform: IaC tool. Allowed: version, validate, fmt, plan, show, state list/show, output. BLOCKED: apply, destroy, import, taint.
- jq/yq: For processing Terraform output and state files.

NOTE: Terraform plan is allowed but apply is blocked for safety.

properties.label: Tool
properties.description: CLI tool to execute
properties.tooltip:
Monitoring and observability tools (read-only):

- kubectl: K8s monitoring (get, describe, logs, top). No mutations.
- curl: HTTP client for health checks and API queries.
- jq: JSON processing for parsing responses.

These tools are restricted to read-only operations for safe monitoring.

properties.label: Script Language
properties.description: Programming language for the script
properties.tooltip:
Select the programming language of your script content.

Currently supported:
- Python: Multi-statement Python scripts with access to safe modules (json, math, datetime, etc.)

More languages coming soon.

properties.label: Script Content
properties.description: Multi-line Python script code to execute
properties.tooltip:
Enter your Python code here. The code will be written to a file and executed safely in the sandbox.

Example:
import json
import math

data = {"result": math.sqrt(2)}
print(json.dumps(data))

SECURITY: Dangerous modules (os, subprocess, socket, etc.) are blocked.
Use FEEL expressions (=prefix) to include process variables.

Output: Print JSON to stdout - it will be captured in sandboxResult.stdout

properties.label: Arguments
properties.description: Command arguments (tool name is added automatically)
properties.tooltip:
Enter ONLY the arguments, NOT the tool name.

Examples:
- jq: '.items[].name' or '-r .status'
- curl: '-s https://api.example.com/health'
- kubectl: 'get pods -n default -o json'
- safe-python3: '-c "print(1+1)"' (for simple one-liners)

SECURITY: Shell operators (|, &, ;, >, <) are blocked.
Use FEEL expressions (=prefix) for dynamic values from process variables.

For multi-statement Python code, use Script mode instead!

properties.label: Input Data (stdin)
properties.description: Data to pass to the command via stdin
properties.tooltip:
Data piped to the command's standard input. Ideal for:

- jq/yq: JSON/YAML to process (e.g., =myProcessVariable)
- grep/sed/awk: Text to search/transform

Use a FEEL expression (=variableName) to pass process variables, or enter literal JSON/text directly.

Leave empty if the command fetches its own data (e.g., curl, kubectl).

properties.label: Environment Variables
properties.description: Additional environment variables as JSON object

properties.label: Network Access
properties.description: Network isolation level
properties.tooltip:
Controls what network destinations the command can reach:

- NONE: No network (default, most secure). For local data processing.
- INTERNAL: Cluster-internal only. For internal service calls.
- RESTRICTED: Only pre-approved hosts (defined in tenant policy).
- FULL: Any destination. Use only when necessary.

Tools like curl, cloud CLIs require at least RESTRICTED or FULL access.

properties.label: Timeout
properties.description: Maximum execution time before termination
properties.tooltip:
How long the command can run before being killed:

- 10s: Quick operations (jq, simple curl)
- 30s: Standard API calls (default)
- 60s: Slower operations (git, kubectl)
- 120s: Cloud CLI operations
- 300s: Long-running tasks (terraform plan)

Exceeding timeout results in an error with code TIMEOUT.

properties.label: Memory Limit
properties.description: Maximum RAM the command can use
properties.tooltip:
Memory cap for the sandboxed process. Exceeding causes OOM kill.

- 128 MB: Text tools (grep, sed, awk)
- 256 MB: JSON/YAML processing (jq, yq)
- 512 MB: Cloud CLIs (aws, gcloud, az)
- 1024 MB: Heavy processing (terraform, large datasets)

If you see OOM errors, increase this limit.

properties.label: Result Variable
properties.description: Process variable to store the full result object
properties.tooltip:
The complete execution result is stored here as an object with these fields:

- stdout: Command output (string)
- stderr: Error output (string)
- exitCode: Exit code (0 = success)
- success: Boolean (true if exitCode is 0)
- executionTimeMs: Duration in milliseconds
- resourceUsage: Memory and CPU used

Access fields like: =sandboxResult.stdout

properties.label: Result Expression
properties.description: FEEL expression to transform the result
properties.tooltip:
Optional FEEL expression to extract/transform data from the result before storing.

Examples:
- Just output: =response.stdout
- Parse JSON output: =response.stdout.jsonParse()
- Custom object: ={data: response.stdout, ok: response.exitCode = 0}
- Handle errors: =if response.success then response.stdout else response.stderr

The full response object is available as 'response'.

properties.label: Error Handling Mode
properties.description: How should CLI execution failures be handled?
properties.tooltip:
Choose how to handle non-zero exit codes from CLI commands:

- IGNORE: Non-zero exit codes don't throw errors (check sandboxResult.success yourself)
- THROW BPMN ERROR: Automatically throw a BPMN error that can be caught by a boundary error event
- CUSTOM: Write your own FEEL expression

properties.value: =if response.exitCode != 0 then bpmnError("CLI_EXECUTION_ERROR", "Exit code " + string(response.exitCode) + ": " + (if response.stderr != null and response.stderr != "" then response.stderr else "Command failed")) else null

properties.label: Error Expression
properties.description: Custom FEEL expression for error handling
properties.tooltip:
Write a FEEL expression to handle errors. The 'response' object contains:
- response.exitCode: Exit code (0 = success)
- response.success: Boolean
- response.stdout: Command output (never null, may be empty)
- response.stderr: Error output (never null, may be empty)

Example to throw BPMN error:
=if response.exitCode != 0 then bpmnError("MY_ERROR", "Failed: " + response.stderr) else null

IMPORTANT: Always handle null - use (response.stderr != null then response.stderr else "")

properties.label: Retries
properties.description: Number of automatic retries on failure
properties.tooltip:
How many times Zeebe should retry this task on failure:

- 0: No retries (fail immediately)
- 1-3: For transient errors (network issues)
- 5: For unreliable external services

Retries use exponential backoff. Non-retryable errors (validation, security) fail immediately regardless of this setting.
